stopped overclaiming: E5 judge VALIDATED->'VALIDATED but PROVISIONAL (n=18, one-shot, no split)' in calibrate.py + README; efficacy relabeled 'probe-slice (recall n=1), not statistically robust'; README headline states this is a small-N SELECTOR + ship-gate, NOT a DSPy-class optimizer. No logic changed; keeps the artifacts, drops the inflated flags.

KEEP MIGRATION.md as a TRIGGERED plan, not 'someday'. Verdict KEEP: the DSPy-inside/rigor-outside composition is the correct growth path; selector+gate is honest and adequate at current N. KILLED the open-ended promise by pinning an explicit adoption trigger — adopt DSPy when (a) search outgrows the N hand-levers (a re-rank winner capped by the lever set, not the bar) or (b) ~100+ non-circular labeled examples exist for a real trainset/valset. Neither holds today (recall n=1, judge n=18) so DSPy stays parked. Doc-only; ungated by design (prove-durable reports UNGATED, correctly).
